You are Archon Horizon's Ground agent: the keeper of the project's human-readable mind. You own three things and must leave each true to the project's real state before you finish:
- the blueprints (LaTeX-subset declarations + `\uses` dependency edges),
- the roadmap (the planning index that drives scheduling) — edit it ONLY via the `"$HORIZON_BIN" roadmap` CLI (`set`/`add`/`remove`, `comment <id> --author ground` to log a key advance; pass long prose with `--summary-file` and `--author` on add/set), NEVER by hand-editing the YAML, which corrupts it; the CLI quotes safely,
- memory: durable facts/conventions/dead ends, kept as `memory` inbox items (`horizon inbox add --kind memory --author ground`); prune stale ones with `inbox complete`.
You do NOT do long Lean proof search yourself. You set strategy, keep the blueprints aligned with both the Lean code and the long-term plan, supervise the Horizon agent, and leave it a clear recommendation of what to attempt next.

The Horizon agent runs free and may leave the workspace messy or its reasoning shaky — you are its supervisor and the workspace's janitor, and you make it correct and tidy again.

How to work:
- Review the Horizon agent's latest work via its report and the project diff (see the `project-git` skill — projects have no root `.git`; diff them through their out-of-tree git). Check that the Lean definitions truly match the blueprint statements, that its reasoning is sound, and that it left no stray files or directories.
- Reconcile blueprints, roadmap status, and memory with what actually changed. Fix blueprint errors, retrieve and add missing references, and keep the DAG dependency-correct.
- When you find a flaw — a wrong proof, a Lean/blueprint mismatch, a dead end — raise an inbox item or comment rather than silently papering over it (see the horizon-inbox skill); use `--author ground` for inbox items/comments you create. ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, roadmap summaries, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. The inbox is durable coordination, not a progress log: prefer one concise issue/hint over many comments, close obsolete items, retag or merge duplicates, and add a concise closing comment when you `complete` an item.
- When you make a change the human should know about — you restructured projects, took an unexpected direction, or hit something worth flagging — tell them with an `info` inbox item (`horizon inbox add --kind info --author ground`). It is purely a notice and never affects what runs.
- You do not create tasks: tasks are the human's way to launch sessions (and are inferred on demand from a roadmap milestone via `horizon run <roadmap_id>`). To propose work, open an inbox item addressed to the human (`horizon inbox add --to human`); you may `horizon task comment` to suggest an edit, but never add/edit/remove tasks.
- Delegate to subagents to divide the work (blueprint review, diff analysis), preferring a smaller/cheaper model for mechanical checks.
- Keep the roadmap as the project's mathematical status: the main theorems and infrastructure already formalized and still to build, each a milestone that guides the work — it is a map, not a task queue. Update item statuses to match the real Lean/blueprint state, and add milestones for major results that emerge. Marking an item active is guidance, not a command, and does not by itself launch work: a human runs a milestone with `horizon run <roadmap_id>`. If you think work should start, say so in an inbox item to the human.
- Treat the opened inbox below as input for this round only. Preserve frozen/protected state; if an edit would violate it, leave it and flag it.

Blueprint constraints (legible and pure, but mathematically complete — see the blueprint-conventions skill):
- One declaration's worth per node: a short statement + a COMPLETE proof (not a sketch). Keep nodes small by splitting a hard step into its own `\uses`-linked lemma, never by abbreviating the proof to a hand-wave.
- Pure mathematics only: no Lean tactics, no semi-Lean pseudocode, no project history. Do not restate Lean source as prose. Add a `\uses` edge rather than re-explaining a dependency.
- The roadmap is an index, not a journal: one line of summary per item; link, don't recount.
- Memory holds only what is non-obvious and durable: conventions, invariants, dead ends.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

# Roadmap
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×2).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- CECH.acyclic [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing) - **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing).**
$$\check{H}^{i}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})=0\ (i>0)\quad\text{on affine opens},$$
via tilde-vanishing $\Rightarrow$ affine Čech vanishing; localization comparison `AwayComparison`, $\varphi/\varphi_L$ naturality.

- CECH.engine [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Combinatorial / free Čech engine - **Combinatorial / free Čech engine (sorry-free).** Alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, and exactness of the augmented Čech complex on a cover.

- CECH.main [done/proof/high] (Cech-Cohomology) cech_computes_higherDirectImage (unconditional) - **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`.**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}$$
for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover — unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

- CECH.pushpull [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) PushPull functoriality - **PushPull functoriality.** `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, and the pentagon for the sigma iso over the Čech nerve / wide pullbacks (finitary-extensive distributivity).

- GRQ.cells [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent - **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent (sorry-free).** Rank-quotient setoid, affine charts, the $\mathrm{GL}_{d}$ transition cocycle, and effective descent gluing the charts into $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$.

- GRQ.graded [blocked/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Section graded ring (SNAP) - **Section graded ring (SNAP, sorry-free).** The graded ring/module lanes (Stacks 01CV) and graded Hilbert–Serre rationality:
$$\sum_{n}\dim_{k}M_{n}\,t^{n}\in\mathbb{Z}(t)\ \text{(rational)}.$$

- GRQ.quot [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent) - **Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent, sorry-free).** The quasi-coherent-descent machinery of `QuotScheme` retained after carving the χ-blocked Hilbert-polynomial endgame (which stays open in the AJC copy).

- GRQ.represents [blocked/proof/high] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian.represents (H^0 leg) - **Representability `Grassmannian.represents` (H^0 leg).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\ \text{represents}\ T\mapsto\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{rank }d\,\},\qquad \texttt{tautologicalQuotient\_epi}.$$

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable — finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices — `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` — the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.

- MR.pic [pending/blueprint/normal] (MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard) Compactifying the Picard scheme (D'Souza) - **Compactifying the Picard scheme** (D'Souza, *MR0555258*). Blueprint-stage related-paper formalization: the compactified Jacobian / degenerate Picard variety of a singular curve. Depends on the core AG engine (schemes, cohomology, Picard). # Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

1	---
2	name: blueprint-conventions
3	description: The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
4	---
5	
6	Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
7	Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.
8	
9	## Environments
10	
11	Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
12	`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
13	`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
14	its following `proof` environment form one node's content.
15	
16	## Per-node macros
17	
18	- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
19	- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
20	  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
21	- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
22	  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
23	  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
24	  proof.
25	- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
26	  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
27	  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
28	- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
29	  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
30	  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
31	- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
32	  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).
33	
34	## House style
35	
36	- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
37	  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
38	  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
39	  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
40	- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
41	  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
42	  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
43	  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
44	  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
45	  its proof stays complete.
46	- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
47	- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
48	  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
49	  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
50	  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

diff --git a/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex b/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex
index 5049541..44b9197 100644
--- a/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex
+++ b/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex
@@ -1258,6 +1258,159 @@ either directly or through the Jacobian regular-sequence witness.
   \cref{lem:ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_regular_sequence}.
 \end{proof}
 
+\paragraph{Closed-point completion of the 00OE chain (Krull height theorem route).}
+
+The lemmas above reduce the localised Krull-dimension formula at a maximal
+ideal to a regular-sequence witness for the relations of the presentation. The
+following route closes the closed-point case unconditionally, replacing that
+witness by Krull's height theorem: the dimension \emph{lower} bound comes from
+the full height of the pulled-back maximal ideal in the polynomial ring, and
+the \emph{upper} bound is automatic from the cotangent space. Combined with the
+cotangent computation \cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue},
+this proves regularity of a standard-smooth algebra at every closed point.
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Maximal ideals of polynomial rings over a field have full height]
+  \label{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
+  \lean{MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
+  For a field \(k\) and a finite index set \(I\), every maximal ideal \(M\) of
+  the polynomial ring \(k[X_i : i \in I]\) has height exactly \(\#I\).
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  Induction on \(\#I\). For \(I = \emptyset\) the ring is the field \(k\), of
+  Krull dimension \(0\), so every prime has height \(0\). For the induction
+  step write \(k[X_i : i \in I \sqcup \{\ast\}] = R[X]\) with
+  \(R = k[X_i : i \in I]\). Given a maximal ideal \(M \subseteq R[X]\), its
+  contraction \(\mathfrak p = M \cap R\) is again maximal, because \(R\) is a
+  finitely generated algebra over a field and hence a Jacobson ring. For a
+  maximal ideal of \(R[X]\) over a Noetherian ring \(R\) one has
+  \(\operatorname{ht} M = \operatorname{ht} \mathfrak p + 1\): the fibre
+  \(R[X] \otimes_R \kappa(\mathfrak p) = \kappa(\mathfrak p)[X]\) is a
+  principal ideal domain, so the image of \(M\) there has height \(1\), and
+  going-down along the flat extension \(R \to R[X]\) splices the chains. The
+  claim follows from the inductive hypothesis
+  \(\operatorname{ht}\mathfrak p = \#I\).
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Height lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}
+  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal}
+  Let \(k\) be a field and \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
+  dimension \(n\). Then every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) has
+  height at least \(n\).
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
+  Choose a submersive presentation \(S = P/I\) with
+  \(P = k[X_1, \dots, X_N]\) and \(I = (f_1, \dots, f_c)\) generated by the
+  \(c\) relations, so that \(n = N - c\); the submersive structure embeds the
+  relation indices into the generator indices, so \(c \le N\) and
+  \(n + c = N\). The preimage \(M \subseteq P\) of \(\mathfrak m\) is a maximal
+  ideal containing \(I\), of height \(N\) by
+  \cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}. Krull's height
+  theorem, in the relative form
+  \(\operatorname{ht} M \le \operatorname{ht}(M/I) + \mu(I)\) where
+  \(\mu(I) \le c\) is the minimal number of generators of \(I\), gives
+  \(N \le \operatorname{ht}(\mathfrak m) + c\) since \(M/I\) corresponds to
+  \(\mathfrak m\) under \(P/I \simeq S\). Hence
+  \(n \le \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\).
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Dimension lower bound at closed points of standard-smooth algebras]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}
+  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime}
+  Let \(k\) be a field, \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
+  dimension \(n\), \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) a maximal ideal, and
+  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) a localisation of \(S\) at \(\mathfrak m\). Then
+  \(n \le \dim S_{\mathfrak m}\).
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
+        lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}
+  Combine \(\dim S_{\mathfrak m} = \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\)
+  (\cref{lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}) with the height
+  lower bound \cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Regularity from the cotangent upper bound]
+  \label{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}
+  \lean{IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim}
+  Let \(A\) be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and
+  residue field \(\kappa\). If
+  \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 \le \dim A\), then \(A\) is a
+  regular local ring.
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  By Nakayama's lemma the minimal number of generators \(\mu(\mathfrak m)\)
+  equals \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2\), and Krull's height theorem
+  gives \(\dim A \le \mu(\mathfrak m)\) in any Noetherian local ring. The
+  hypothesis forces \(\mu(\mathfrak m) = \dim A\), which is the definition of
+  regularity.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Stacks 00TT at a rational closed point, algebra level]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue}
+  Let \(k\) be a field, \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra,
+  \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) a maximal ideal, and \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) a
+  localisation of \(S\) at \(\mathfrak m\) such that the composite
+  \(k \to S_{\mathfrak m} \to \kappa(\mathfrak m)\) is bijective (a
+  \(k\)-rational point). Then \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is a regular local ring.
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
+        lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim,
+        lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue,
+        lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth,
+        lem:module_free_kaehler_localization,
+        lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
+        lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}
+  Let \(n\) be the relative dimension of \(S\) over \(k\)
+  (\cref{lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}).
+  The ring \(S\) is Noetherian (finite type over a field), hence so is
+  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\), and \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is formally smooth over \(k\)
+  (a localisation of a smooth algebra). Its K\"ahler module
+  \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak m}/k}\) is free of rank \(n\)
+  (\cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth},
+  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
+  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}), so by the conormal
+  computation at the \(k\)-rational point
+  (\cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue})
+  \(\dim_{\kappa} \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 = n\). Since
+  \(n \le \dim S_{\mathfrak m}\)
+  (\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}), regularity
+  follows from \cref{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Stacks 00TT at closed points over an algebraically closed field]
+  \label{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed}
+  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field and \(S\) a standard-smooth
+  \(\bar k\)-algebra. Then for every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\)
+  the local ring \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is regular.
+\end{lemma}
+
+\begin{proof}
+  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}
+  By Zariski's lemma the residue field \(S/\mathfrak m\) is a finite, hence
+  algebraic, extension of \(\bar k\); as \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed,
+  \(\bar k \to S/\mathfrak m\) is bijective, and composing with the
+  isomorphism \(S/\mathfrak m \simeq \kappa(\mathfrak m)\) (valid since
+  \(\mathfrak m\) is maximal) shows every maximal ideal is a
+  \(\bar k\)-rational point. Conclude with
+  \cref{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}.
+\end{proof}

74	    (M : Ideal (MvPolynomial ι k)) (hM : M.IsMaximal) :
75	    M.height = Nat.card ι := by

78	    haveI := hM
79	    have h1 := IH (M.comap (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k e).toRingEquiv)
80	      (Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_equiv _)
81	    rw [RingEquiv.height_comap] at h1
82	    rw [h1, Nat.card_congr e]

88	      hdim ▸ Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hM.ne_top
89	    have hz : M.height = 0 := le_antisymm (by exact_mod_cast hle) (by simp)
90	    simp [hz]

92	    rename_i α _

95	    set P' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial α k)) := M.map ψ with hP'
96	    haveI hP'max : P'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv ψ
97	    set p : Ideal (MvPolynomial α k) := P'.comap (Polynomial.C) with hpdef

118	
119	Proof: extract a submersive presentation `P` (with `#ι` generators, `#σ`
120	relations, `n = #ι - #σ`). The pullback `M` of `m` to the polynomial ring
121	`P.Ring` is maximal of height `#ι`
122	(`MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`), contains the kernel of the
123	presentation (generated by the `#σ` relations), and Krull's height theorem
124	(`Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`) gives
125	`#ι = ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`, whence `n ≤ ht m`. -/
126	theorem natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal
127	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
128	    [H : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
129	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
130	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, hσ, hι, P, hPdim⟩ := H.out
131	  haveI := hm
132	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P.Ring S) := P.algebraMap_surjective
133	  set M : Ideal P.Ring := m.comap (algebraMap P.Ring S) with hMdef
134	  haveI hM : M.IsMaximal := Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ hsurj
135	  -- full height of the pullback in the polynomial ring
136	  have hMheight : M.height = Nat.card ι := MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal M hM
137	  -- the presentation kernel sits inside the pullback
138	  have hker_le : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) ≤ M := fun x hx => by
139	    rw [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap, RingHom.mem_ker.mp hx]
140	    exact m.zero_mem
141	  -- Krull's height theorem
142	  have hbound := Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le hker_le
143	  -- identify the quotient-side prime with `m` (first isomorphism theorem)
144	  set e := (algebraMap P.Ring S : P.Ring →+* S).quotientKerEquivOfSurjective hsurj with hedef
145	  have hMcomap : M = Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))
146	      (Ideal.comap e m) := by
147	    ext x
148	    simp only [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap]
149	    rw [hedef, RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk]
150	  have hqheight : (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
151	      = m.height := by
152	    rw [hMcomap, Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective,
153	      RingEquiv.height_comap]
154	  -- the kernel is generated by the `#σ` relations
155	  have hker_span : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) = Ideal.span (Set.range P.relation) :=
156	    P.span_range_relation_eq_ker.symm
157	  have hfr : (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank ≤ Nat.card σ := by
158	    rw [hker_span]
159	    refine le_trans (Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_range _)) ?_
160	    calc (Set.range P.relation).ncard
161	        = (P.relation '' Set.univ).ncard := by rw [Set.image_univ]
162	      _ ≤ (Set.univ : Set σ).ncard := Set.ncard_image_le Set.finite_univ
163	      _ = Nat.card σ := Set.ncard_univ σ
164	  -- cardinal bookkeeping: `n + #σ = #ι`
165	  have hcards : n + Nat.card σ = Nat.card ι := by
166	    have hle : Nat.card σ ≤ Nat.card ι := Nat.card_le_card_of_injective P.map P.map_inj
167	    have hdim : Nat.card ι - Nat.card σ = n := hPdim
168	    omega
169	  -- combine
170	  have h1 : (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
171	    calc (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) = M.height := hMheight.symm
172	      _ ≤ (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
173	          + (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank := hbound
174	      _ ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
175	          rw [hqheight]
176	          have hfr' : ((RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank : ℕ∞)
177	              ≤ (Nat.card σ : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hfr
178	          exact add_le_add le_rfl hfr'
179	  rw [← hcards] at h1
180	  push_cast at h1
181	  exact (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by simp)).mp h1
182	
183	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound, localised form.** For a standard-smooth algebra
184	`S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k`, a maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` and a
185	localisation `Sₘ` of `S` at `m`, the Krull dimension of `Sₘ` is at least `n`. -/
186	theorem le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
187	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
188	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
189	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
190	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m] :
191	    (n : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ := by
192	  haveI := hm.isPrime
193	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m Sₘ]
194	  exact_mod_cast natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal (k := k) n m hm
195	
196	end Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
197	
198	end StandardSmoothLowerBound
199	
200	section RegularityGlue
201	
202	/-- **Regularity from the cotangent upper bound.** A Noetherian local ring whose
203	cotangent-space dimension is bounded above by its Krull dimension is a regular
204	local ring (the reverse inequality `dim ≤ dim_κ m/m²` holds in any Noetherian
205	local ring, via `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`). This is the glue
206	between the sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation and the sub-gap (ii.B)
207	dimension lower bound in the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline. -/
208	theorem IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
209	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
210	    (h : (Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A) (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace A) : WithBot ℕ∞)
211	      ≤ ringKrullDim A) : IsRegularLocalRing A := by
212	  apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
213	  rwa [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
214	
215	end RegularityGlue

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Krull dimension at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras (Stacks 00OE substrate)
10	
11	This file discharges **sub-gap (ii.B)** of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline of
12	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (Stacks tag `00OE`, the smooth-algebra
13	Krull-dimension formula), in the closed-point form the pipeline consumes: for a
14	standard-smooth algebra `S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k` and a
15	*maximal* ideal `m ⊆ S`, the localisation `Sₘ` has Krull dimension (at least,
16	hence — combined with the cotangent bound — exactly) `n`.
17	
18	Contrary to the iter-199 estimate ("~200–300 LOC on `transcendenceDegree` +
19	Noether normalisation"), no transcendence-degree theory is needed: the v4.31
20	Mathlib toolbox (`Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`, Krull's height theorem
21	in the form `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`, and the
22	`MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance) supports a direct height computation:
23	
24	* `MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`: every maximal ideal of
25	  `MvPolynomial ι k` (finitely many variables over a field) has height
26	  `Nat.card ι`. Induction over `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`; the
27	  induction step contracts a maximal ideal of `R[X]` to a maximal ideal of `R`
28	  (Jacobson) and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`.
29	* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal`:
30	  the **lower bound** `n ≤ ht m` for a maximal ideal `m` of a standard-smooth
31	  `k`-algebra of relative dimension `n`. Writing `S = P.Ring ⧸ ker` for a
32	  submersive presentation `P` with `#ι` generators and `#σ` relations
33	  (`n = #ι - #σ`), the pullback `M ⊆ P.Ring` of `m` is maximal of height `#ι`,
34	  and Krull's height theorem bounds `ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`.
35	* `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`:
36	  the same lower bound for `ringKrullDim Sₘ` at the localisation.
37	* `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`: the generic
38	  regularity glue — a Noetherian local ring whose cotangent-space dimension is
39	  bounded by its Krull dimension is regular (the reverse inequality is
40	  automatic).
41	
42	The consumer (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` Stage 6 in
43	`CodimOneExtension.lean`) combines the last two lemmas with the iter-199
44	sub-gap (ii.A) helper `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
45	(`finrank κ(m) (m/m²) = n` at a `k`-rational closed point) to conclude
46	regularity of the stalk at closed points. The remaining gap for *non-closed*
47	points is Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular),
48	tracked in `CodimOneExtension.lean`.
49	
50	Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`
51	(nodes `lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`,
52	`lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal`,
53	`lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization`,
54	`lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim`).
55	-/
56	
57	universe u v
58	
59	open Ideal
60	
61	section MvPolynomialHeight
62	
63	/-- **Maximal ideals of finite-variable polynomial rings over a field have full
64	height.** For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι` and a maximal ideal
65	`M ⊆ k[Xᵢ : i ∈ ι]`, the height of `M` is `Nat.card ι`.
66	
67	Induction over `ι` via `Finite.induction_empty_option`: the base case is the
68	field `k` itself (Krull dimension `0`), and the induction step writes
69	`MvPolynomial (Option α) k ≃+* (MvPolynomial α k)[X]`, contracts the maximal
70	ideal along `C` (maximal again, since `MvPolynomial α k` is Jacobson), and
71	applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`. -/
72	theorem MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal
73	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ι : Type v} [Finite ι]
74	    (M : Ideal (MvPolynomial ι k)) (hM : M.IsMaximal) :
75	    M.height = Nat.card ι := by
76	  induction ι using Finite.induction_empty_option with
77	  | of_equiv e IH =>
78	    haveI := hM
79	    have h1 := IH (M.comap (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k e).toRingEquiv)
80	      (Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_equiv _)
81	    rw [RingEquiv.height_comap] at h1

102	    have hMP' : M.height = P'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map ψ M).symm

104	      simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]

111	section StandardSmoothLowerBound
112	
113	namespace Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
114	
115	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound (closed-point form).** For a standard-smooth
116	algebra `S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k` and a maximal ideal
117	`m ⊆ S`, the height of `m` is at least `n`.
118	
119	Proof: extract a submersive presentation `P` (with `#ι` generators, `#σ`
120	relations, `n = #ι - #σ`). The pullback `M` of `m` to the polynomial ring
121	`P.Ring` is maximal of height `#ι`
122	(`MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal`), contains the kernel of the
123	presentation (generated by the `#σ` relations), and Krull's height theorem
124	(`Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le`) gives
125	`#ι = ht M ≤ ht m + #σ`, whence `n ≤ ht m`. -/
126	theorem natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal
127	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
128	    [H : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
129	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
130	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, hσ, hι, P, hPdim⟩ := H.out
131	  haveI := hm
132	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P.Ring S) := P.algebraMap_surjective
133	  set M : Ideal P.Ring := m.comap (algebraMap P.Ring S) with hMdef
134	  haveI hM : M.IsMaximal := Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ hsurj
135	  -- full height of the pullback in the polynomial ring
136	  have hMheight : M.height = Nat.card ι := MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal M hM
137	  -- the presentation kernel sits inside the pullback
138	  have hker_le : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) ≤ M := fun x hx => by
139	    rw [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap, RingHom.mem_ker.mp hx]
140	    exact m.zero_mem
141	  -- Krull's height theorem
142	  have hbound := Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le hker_le
143	  -- identify the quotient-side prime with `m` (first isomorphism theorem)
144	  set e := (algebraMap P.Ring S : P.Ring →+* S).quotientKerEquivOfSurjective hsurj with hedef
145	  have hMcomap : M = Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))
146	      (Ideal.comap e m) := by
147	    ext x
148	    simp only [hMdef, Ideal.mem_comap]
149	    rw [hedef, RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk]
150	  have hqheight : (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
151	      = m.height := by
152	    rw [hMcomap, Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective,
153	      RingEquiv.height_comap]
154	  -- the kernel is generated by the `#σ` relations
155	  have hker_span : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S) = Ideal.span (Set.range P.relation) :=
156	    P.span_range_relation_eq_ker.symm
157	  have hfr : (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank ≤ Nat.card σ := by
158	    rw [hker_span]
159	    refine le_trans (Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_range _)) ?_
160	    calc (Set.range P.relation).ncard
161	        = (P.relation '' Set.univ).ncard := by rw [Set.image_univ]
162	      _ ≤ (Set.univ : Set σ).ncard := Set.ncard_image_le Set.finite_univ
163	      _ = Nat.card σ := Set.ncard_univ σ
164	  -- cardinal bookkeeping: `n + #σ = #ι`
165	  have hcards : n + Nat.card σ = Nat.card ι := by
166	    have hle : Nat.card σ ≤ Nat.card ι := Nat.card_le_card_of_injective P.map P.map_inj
167	    have hdim : Nat.card ι - Nat.card σ = n := hPdim
168	    omega
169	  -- combine
170	  have h1 : (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
171	    calc (Nat.card ι : ℕ∞) = M.height := hMheight.symm
172	      _ ≤ (M.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)))).height
173	          + (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank := hbound
174	      _ ≤ m.height + Nat.card σ := by
175	          rw [hqheight]
176	          have hfr' : ((RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)).spanFinrank : ℕ∞)
177	              ≤ (Nat.card σ : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hfr
178	          exact add_le_add le_rfl hfr'
179	  rw [← hcards] at h1
180	  push_cast at h1
181	  exact (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by simp)).mp h1
182	
183	/-- **Stacks 00OE lower bound, localised form.** For a standard-smooth algebra
184	`S` of relative dimension `n` over a field `k`, a maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` and a
185	localisation `Sₘ` of `S` at `m`, the Krull dimension of `Sₘ` is at least `n`. -/
186	theorem le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
187	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra k S] (n : ℕ)
188	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S]
189	    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
190	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m] :
191	    (n : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ := by
192	  haveI := hm.isPrime
193	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m Sₘ]
194	  exact_mod_cast natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal (k := k) n m hm
195	
196	end Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
197	
198	end StandardSmoothLowerBound

200	section RegularityGlue
201	
202	/-- **Regularity from the cotangent upper bound.** A Noetherian local ring whose
203	cotangent-space dimension is bounded above by its Krull dimension is a regular
204	local ring (the reverse inequality `dim ≤ dim_κ m/m²` holds in any Noetherian
205	local ring, via `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`). This is the glue
206	between the sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation and the sub-gap (ii.B)
207	dimension lower bound in the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline. -/
208	theorem IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
209	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
210	    (h : (Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A) (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace A) : WithBot ℕ∞)
211	      ≤ ringKrullDim A) : IsRegularLocalRing A := by
212	  apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
213	  rwa [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
214	
215	end RegularityGlue

130	
131	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed

140

150	
151	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
152	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
153	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
154	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
155	`indeterminacyLocus f`.

161	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
162	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
163	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
164	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain

import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.StandardSmoothDimension
/-- **Lane COE Step B.d — Stacks `00TT` at a rational closed point, algebra
level (this iter).** For a standard-smooth algebra `S` over a field `k`, a
maximal ideal `m ⊆ S` whose induced residue map `k → κ(m)` is bijective (the
`k`-rational-point condition, automatic over an algebraically closed field by
the Nullstellensatz), the localisation `Sₘ` is a regular local ring.

This *closes sub-gap (ii.B) at closed points*: the proof combines
* the iter-199 sub-gap (ii.A) cotangent computation
  `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
  (`finrank κ (m/m²) = n`, from formal smoothness + free Kähler differentials
  of rank `n`), with
* the new `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` dimension lower bound
  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`
  (`n ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ`, via the polynomial-ring height computation and
  Krull's height theorem — no transcendence-degree theory needed), through
* the generic glue `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`
  (a Noetherian local ring with `dim_κ m/m² ≤ ringKrullDim` is regular).

The consumer `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` still carries its `sorry`
because its statement quantifies over *all* points `z`: at a non-closed point
the residue field is a transcendental extension of `k̄` and the bijectivity
hypothesis fails; localising this closed-point witness to a general point is
Stacks `00OF` (localisations of regular local rings are regular), the one
remaining genuine Mathlib gap of the pipeline. Axiom-clean. -/
theorem isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal)
    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra k Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower k S Sₘ] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sₘ m]
    (hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))) :
    IsRegularLocalRing Sₘ := by
  haveI := hm.isPrime
  -- Noetherian structure: standard-smooth ⟹ finite presentation ⟹ Noetherian
  -- over the base field; localisation preserves Noetherianness.
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing S := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing k S
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Sₘ := IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing m.primeCompl Sₘ ‹_›
  -- Extract the relative dimension n.
  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ :=
    exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth (R := k) (S := S)
  haveI := hn
  -- Kähler package at the localisation (Stages 4, 5a, 5b).
  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄k]) := module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
  haveI : Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄k]) :=
    module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization m.primeCompl Sₘ
  have hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄k]) = n :=
    rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension n m.primeCompl Sₘ
  -- Formal smoothness of the localisation over k.
  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth S Sₘ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization m.primeCompl
  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth k Sₘ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.comp k S Sₘ
  -- (ii.A): cotangent finrank = n at the k-rational closed point.
  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue hbij n hrank
  -- (ii.B): dimension lower bound n ≤ dim Sₘ.
  have hdim :=
    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime
      (k := k) n m hm Sₘ
  exact IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
    (by rw [hcot]; exact_mod_cast hdim)

/-- **Lane COE Step B.d′ — Stacks `00TT` at closed points over an algebraically
closed field, unconditional form.** Over an algebraically closed base field the
`k`-rationality hypothesis of Step B.d is automatic: for any maximal ideal
`m ⊆ S` of the standard-smooth algebra `S`, the residue field `S ⧸ m` is a
finite (Zariski's lemma, `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing`) hence
algebraic extension of `k`, so `k → κ(m)` is bijective
(`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` composed with
`Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`). Hence the local ring of a
standard-smooth `k̄`-algebra at **every** closed point is regular. Stated for
the model localisation `Localization.AtPrime m`; transport to an abstract
localisation (e.g. a scheme stalk) via `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv` along
`IsLocalization.algEquiv`. Axiom-clean. -/
theorem isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed
    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
    (m : Ideal S) (hm : m.IsMaximal) :
    IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime m) := by
  haveI := hm.isPrime
  refine isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue
    (k := k) m hm (Localization.AtPrime m) ?_
  -- Zariski's lemma: the residue field `S ⧸ m` is module-finite over `k`.
  -- (The `Field` instance must be installed FIRST so all subsequent instance
  -- searches on `S ⧸ m` share its semiring key.)
  letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m
  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType k (S ⧸ m) :=
    Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ k m) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  haveI : Module.Finite k (S ⧸ m) := finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing k (S ⧸ m)
  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k (S ⧸ m) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k (S ⧸ m)
  -- `k → S ⧸ m` is bijective since `k` is algebraically closed.
  have h1 : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (S ⧸ m)) :=
    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
  -- `S ⧸ m → κ(m)` is bijective since `m` is maximal.
  have h2 := m.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField
  rw [show algebraMap k (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (Localization.AtPrime m))
      = (algebraMap (S ⧸ m) m.ResidueField).comp (algebraMap k (S ⧸ m)) from
    IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq k (S ⧸ m) m.ResidueField]
  exact h2.comp h1

* **Stage 6 sub-gap (ii.B) DISCHARGED (this iter)** — Smooth-algebra
  Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE), closed-point form: the new module
  `Albanese/StandardSmoothDimension.lean` proves
  `MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal` (maximal ideals of finite
  polynomial rings over a field have full height) and the lower bound
  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime`
  (`n ≤ ringKrullDim Sₘ` at any maximal `m`) via Krull's height theorem —
  no transcendence-degree theory needed. Combined with (ii.A) in
  `isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue`
  (§3.B Step B.d) and its unconditional k̄-form
  `isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed`
  (B.d′), the **closed-point case of Stacks 00TT is now proved axiom-clean**:
  the local ring of a standard-smooth `k̄`-algebra at every *maximal* ideal
  is regular.
* Stage 6 residual gap (the reason this helper still carries a `sorry`):
  the statement quantifies over ALL points `z`, and at a **non-closed** `z`
  the prime `primeIdealOf z` is not maximal: the residue field is a
  transcendental extension of `k̄` (so (ii.A)'s bijectivity hypothesis
  genuinely fails — indeed `finrank κ (CotangentSpace Sₘ) = ht z ≠ n` there),
  and the closed-point witness must instead be **localised**: Stacks `00OF`
  (localisations of regular local rings are regular, via Serre's homological
  characterisation) is the one remaining genuine Mathlib gap. Every
  downstream consumer (`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`,
  `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`) inherits the closure automatically once
  00OF lands.
  -- **Residual Stage 6 gap (this-iter surface).** Sub-gaps (i), (ii.A) AND
  -- (ii.B) are now all DISCHARGED axiom-clean
  -- (`exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth`,
  -- `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`, and the new
  -- `Albanese/StandardSmoothDimension.lean` +
  -- `isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue`
  -- / `isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed`
  -- in §3.B, which together prove the **closed-point case** of this helper:
  -- the local ring of a standard-smooth k̄-algebra at every MAXIMAL ideal is
  -- regular).
  -- What remains for THIS statement (arbitrary `z`) is the **non-closed
  -- point** case. There `(hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal` is a non-maximal
  -- prime: the residue field κ(z) has positive transcendence degree over k̄,
  -- so (ii.A)'s bijectivity hypothesis genuinely fails (correctly so:
  -- `finrank κ (CotangentSpace Sₘ) = ht z ≠ n` there — the conormal sequence
  -- acquires the `Ω[κ(z)⁄k̄]` quotient). The classical route localises the
  -- closed-point regularity witness:
  -- **Stacks 00OF — localisations of regular local rings are regular** (via
  -- Serre's homological characterisation `regular ⟺ finite global
  -- dimension`, Stacks 00O7/00OE-part-2). Neither direction of Serre's
  -- characterisation exists in Mathlib at v4.31 (`IsRegularLocalRing` has
  -- only the `RegularLocalRing/Defs.lean` spanFinrank definition layer), so
  -- this is the one remaining genuine Mathlib gap of the pipeline.
  -- **Closure pattern (post-00OF).** Pick a closed specialisation `x` of `z`
  -- in `V` (exists: `V` affine ⟹ quasi-compact sober); then
  -- `stalk z ≅ (stalk x)_{q}` for the prime `q` of `z`, `stalk x` is regular
  -- by the §3.B closed-point theorem (transported along

1272	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1273	[Maximal ideals of polynomial rings over a field have full height]
1274	  \label{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
1275	  \lean{MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
1276	  For a field \(k\) and a finite index set \(I\), every maximal ideal \(M\) of
1277	  the polynomial ring \(k[X_i : i \in I]\) has height exactly \(\#I\).
1278	\end{lemma}
1279	
1280	\begin{proof}
1281	  Induction on \(\#I\). For \(I = \emptyset\) the ring is the field \(k\), of
1282	  Krull dimension \(0\), so every prime has height \(0\). For the induction
1283	  step write \(k[X_i : i \in I \sqcup \{\ast\}] = R[X]\) with
1284	  \(R = k[X_i : i \in I]\). Given a maximal ideal \(M \subseteq R[X]\), its
1285	  contraction \(\mathfrak p = M \cap R\) is again maximal, because \(R\) is a
1286	  finitely generated algebra over a field and hence a Jacobson ring. For a
1287	  maximal ideal of \(R[X]\) over a Noetherian ring \(R\) one has
1288	  \(\operatorname{ht} M = \operatorname{ht} \mathfrak p + 1\): the fibre
1289	  \(R[X] \otimes_R \kappa(\mathfrak p) = \kappa(\mathfrak p)[X]\) is a
1290	  principal ideal domain, so the image of \(M\) there has height \(1\), and
1291	  going-down along the flat extension \(R \to R[X]\) splices the chains. The
1292	  claim follows from the inductive hypothesis
1293	  \(\operatorname{ht}\mathfrak p = \#I\).
1294	\end{proof}
1295	
1296	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1297	[Height lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras]
1298	  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}
1299	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal}
1300	  Let \(k\) be a field and \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
1301	  dimension \(n\). Then every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) has
1302	  height at least \(n\).
1303	\end{lemma}
1304	
1305	\begin{proof}
1306	  \uses{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
1307	  Choose a submersive presentation \(S = P/I\) with
1308	  \(P = k[X_1, \dots, X_N]\) and \(I = (f_1, \dots, f_c)\) generated by the
1309	  \(c\) relations, so that \(n = N - c\); the submersive structure embeds the
1310	  relation indices into the generator indices, so \(c \le N\) and
1311	  \(n + c = N\). The preimage \(M \subseteq P\) of \(\mathfrak m\) is a maximal
1312	  ideal containing \(I\), of height \(N\) by
1313	  \cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}. Krull's height
1314	  theorem, in the relative form
1315	  \(\operatorname{ht} M \le \operatorname{ht}(M/I) + \mu(I)\) where
1316	  \(\mu(I) \le c\) is the minimal number of generators of \(I\), gives
1317	  \(N \le \operatorname{ht}(\mathfrak m) + c\) since \(M/I\) corresponds to
1318	  \(\mathfrak m\) under \(P/I \simeq S\). Hence
1319	  \(n \le \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\).
1320	\end{proof}

1330	\end{lemma}
